source_jobs.title: Sales Development Associate
source_jobs.description:
Artsy is seeking a driven, self-motivated Sales Development Associate to join our Gallery Partnerships team. This sales role is a unique opportunity to contribute to Artsy’s digital transformation of the art world by driving growth and expanding our global gallery network.

As a Sales Development Associate, you will be responsible for identifying and reaching out to prospective galleries, pitching Artsy’s value proposition, and generating new business opportunities. Your primary focus will be building a strong pipeline by engaging potential partners and scheduling meetings for our sales team.

This role sits at the heart of our Gallery Partnerships team, playing a key part in expanding our subscription services and driving revenue growth. By helping galleries connect with a broader audience through Artsy’s platform, you’ll directly support our mission of expanding the art market to support more art and artists in the world.

This is a hybrid role with office attendance required three days per week.




WHAT YOU’LL DO AT ARTSY:

 - Generate outbound leads via cold calling and email campaigns

 - Research and identify newly opened galleries, and galleries not currently known to Artsy to outreach

 - Follow up on marketing generated leads and conduct research to evaluate partnership opportunity and fit

 - Effectively communicate Artsy’s value proposition in sales outreach to galleries to generate interest in the Artsy platform

 - Qualify warm leads for Sales Managers, and schedule product demonstrations with relevant prospects

 - Test, measure, and improve outreach campaign effectiveness and end-to-end lead management

 - Meet and exceed core KPIs: daily dials, meetings booked, and activations




THIS ROLE IS A FIT FOR YOU IF:

 - You are currently authorized to live and work in the US

 - This role will be based in New York City

 - You are interested in sales and being a part of a larger sales team

 - You thrive on challenges and driven to exceed goals

 - You are curious, interested in learning quickly and growing

 - You succeed in a fast-paced, metric-driven role and team

 - You are a compelling and persuasive communicator; comfortable managing communication and outreach across a large target client-base

 - You have strong time management skills

 - You have experience using AI tools such as ChatGPT or other automation tools

It’s a plus if you if you…

 - Can fluently speak other languages; French, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, or Japanese

 - Have worked with Salesforce and/or Outreach

 - You have professional experience or a network within the commercial art world in Europe

This role is unlikely to be a good match if you….

 - You are not comfortable cold calling

 - Prefer to work outside of a team setting

 - Are not interested in a sales, target-heavy role

 - Are not interested in art or the art market

We know no one is a perfect match on paper, so if you’re passionate about this role, please apply! We’re excited to learn how your skills, experiences, and insights could make a difference.




WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Opportunity to reimagine the art industry while working with a talented, diverse, international team. 

 - Competitive salary and equity

 - Flexible time off: our culture of trust and empowerment allows employees to take the time they need away from work while still excelling in their roles

 - We are a global hybrid team: most employees work from our NYC, London, or Berlin offices 2-3 days per week. (In-person expectations vary by role)

 - Comprehensive healthcare & benefit options, including: medical, dental, parental leave, short- and long-term disability, Employee Assistance Program featuring free mental health support, 401k, commuter benefits, flexible spending accounts (FSA), health savings accounts (HSA), financial wellness support & resources, and more

 - Professional development, including: mentoring, lunch & learns, regular training, 1:1 management, and an open feedback culture

 - Engagi

source_jobs.title: Lead Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
Artsy is on a mission to expand the art market to support more artists and art in the world. As a Lead Product Designer, you’ll play a key role in shaping and elevating our customer experience, ensuring collectors and newcomers can seamlessly discover, follow, and purchase artworks. This role requires strategic, user-centered thinking and exceptional design craft to deliver engaging, frictionless experiences that help position Artsy as essential to people collecting art.

You’ll work on complex design challenges that directly impact how people first encounter Artsy and the art world, designing experiences that move casual browsers into engaged collectors. This is an exciting opportunity to drive innovative design solutions that expand our reach beyond traditional art market participants.

You will be part of Product Design, a collaborative group of designers embedded within a multidisciplinary Product organization. People in this group work as part of a team alongside Product, Data, and Engineering to drive user-centered decisions and deliver meaningful impact. Reporting to the Senior Director of Product Design, you will contribute to shaping the product roadmap, developing design solutions to high-impact problems of varying certainty—driving iterative, test-and-learn approaches in ambiguous problem spaces, while confidently accelerating toward focused outcomes in more established domains. You will also collaborate with peers to develop, evolve and continuously improve our design practice and infrastructure.




ROLE OVERVIEW

We're looking for a Lead Product Designer to transform Artsy from a destination for existing art enthusiasts into the discovery portal for new collectors. This role combines strategic thinking with hands-on craft, sitting at the intersection of customer acquisition, brand building, and AI-powered personalisation; designing and delivering the top-of-funnel experiences that introduce people to the art world.

You'll be designing for two critical audiences: existing art world participants who discover us through SEO, and future collectors who don't yet know they're interested in art. This is about shifting from tactical SEO optimisation to strategic traffic and acquisition thinking.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN AT ARTSY

Design-led customer acquisition

 - Transform high-traffic pages from conversion-focused to discovery-focused experiences

 - Design acquisition strategies for audiences currently outside the art world

 - Partner with marketing to create cohesive brand experiences beyond traditional product boundaries

 - Bringing qualitative and quantitative data together to continuously improve the customer experience

Discovery and journey design

 - Design experiences that capture and nurture different stages of art interest and knowledge

 - Create seamless transitions between discovery, education, and transaction moments

 - Build systems that guide people into art collecting regardless of their starting point

AI-accelerated design practice

 - Experiment with AI tools to accelerate design iteration and testing cycles

 - Build rapid prototypes to test how AI-augmented content and summaries might affect user engagement patterns

 - Design and build experiments to help us learn what information drives discovery versus conversion

Brand building through product

 - Ensure product experiences reinforce brand positioning for unfamiliar users

 - Create cohesive narratives that position Artsy as the definitive art discovery platform

 - Help develop and evolve visual brand identity, product UI and design system in collaboration with the other designers, Brand & Engineering teams




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS FOR ARTSY

We're at an inflection point. We’ve built a strong infrastructure for the existing art market, but our biggest growth opportunity lies in expanding the market itself; introducing new people to art collecting and making the art world more accessible.

This role is critical to Artsy because it helps us d

source_jobs.title: Sales Manager, Gallery Partnerships (London)
source_jobs.description:
Artsy is looking for a French or Spanish speaking Sales Representative who will engage with Galleries across the EMEA region, educating and consulting them on their online presence and digital strategy, and ultimately winning new business for Artsy.

This role is a part of our New Business Team, responsible for growing our marketplace supply by bringing new galleries onto Artsy’s platform. This is a target-oriented team where your contributions have a direct impact on Artsy’s bottom line.

In this quota-carrying sales position, you will be responsible for the full sales cycle, pitching Artsy's value proposition to Galleries and driving subscription revenue. You will focus on selling to emerging and Blue-Chip Galleries, requiring art industry expertise and a deep understanding of gallery sales motions and operations.

Working on the New Business Team provides a unique opportunity to expand Artsy’s global gallery network, supporting our goals of expanding the art market to support more artists and art in the world.

This is a hybrid role with office attendance required three days per week.




WHAT YOU’LL DO AT ARTSY:

 - Drive business development efforts by devising, implementing and conducting effective lead generation strategies, engaging with high-value prospects

 - Consistently achieve and surpass monthly and quarterly revenue targets for new gallery acquisitions

 - Manage the end-to-end sales cycle, from initial outreach to closing deals, while maintaining a strong focus on building and nurturing long-term relationships with key prospects

 - Build and oversee a robust sales pipeline, utilizing Salesforce and Outreach, and ensure high conversion rates for both inbound and outbound leads

 - Demonstrate expertise in objection handling, effectively addressing client concerns and positioning Artsy as the premier solution for their needs

 - Cultivate trust and credibility through timely and personalized follow-up, fostering strong and enduring partnerships with prospects

 - Collaborating with the Sales Development Associate to strategize on which markets to target based on events, art happenings and seasonality

 - Utilize AI and automation tools to increase process efficiency and provide better solutions to partners




THIS ROLE IS A FIT FOR YOU IF:

 - You are currently authorized to live and work in the UK

 - Can work three days per week in our London office

 - You are fluent in French and/or Spanish

 - ~5 years of art industry experience in a revenue driving, client acquisition/retention, or account management role

 - You have direct knowledge and understanding of Gallery sales motions

 - You are a consultative sales professional who prioritizes understanding and meeting customer needs in every interaction

 - You are solutions-focused and a creative problem solver, with strong negotiation skills

 - You are highly organized and able to manage sales pipeline efficiently and effectively; strong time management skills

 - You are an effective verbal and written communicator with the ability to effectively engage with VIP clients

 - Metrics-driven and highly results-oriented

 - Affinity for and interest in using AI and automation tools

This role is unlikely to be a good match if you….

 - You are not comfortable with sales, pitching, and negotiating

 - Prefer to work outside of a team setting

 - Are not interested in a target-heavy role

 - Are not interested in art or the art market

We know no one is a perfect match on paper, so if you’re passionate about this role, please apply! We’re excited to learn how your skills, experiences, and insights could make a difference.




WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Opportunity to reimagine the art industry while working with a talented, diverse, international team.

 - Competitive salary and equity

 - Flexible time off: our culture of trust and empowerment allows employees to take the time they need away from work while still excelling in their roles

 - We are a global hybrid tea

source_jobs.title: Sales Lead, New Business (London)
source_jobs.description:
We are seeking a results-driven sales leader to drive sales excellence and consistent target attainment for New Business acquisition across Artsy’s Galleries business.

In this role, you will drive Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) growth and increase new gallery logos by closely monitoring sales KPIs, delivering consistent hands-on coaching, and enforcing rigorous pipeline and forecasting discipline. You will also play a critical role in evolving and scaling cross-product qualification and acquisition motions as our offerings continue to grow.

This is a pivotal moment for the Galleries business. You will be responsible for consistently meeting or exceeding monthly activation targets while nurturing a high-performing team grounded in accountability, consultative selling, and disciplined execution. This role reports to the Senior Director of Business Development.

This role can be either based out of our NYC or London office.

What You’ll Do:

 - Manage and develop a team of 5+ Sales Representatives globally

 - Consistently deliver against new business revenue and new logo acquisition targets.

 - Continue to hone and evolve our sales motions by equipping the team with pitch materials, objection-handling frameworks, and talk tracks across an expanding suite of offerings.

 - Provide consistent sales coaching and address performance gaps directly and promptly.

 - Run disciplined pipeline and forecasting cadences to ensure we have consistent, actionable insights into how we can improve across the full funnel.

 - Monitor leading and lagging KPIs; identify misses and implement solutions to improve conversion, win rates, and velocity.

 - Partner closely with the SDA team to strengthen new prospect identification, lead qualification, handoff, and pipeline generation.

 - Collaborate with B2B Marketing to evolve campaigns in line with the broader strategy.

 - Leverage AI tools and workflow automation to eliminate repetitive work and improve sales quality

What are we looking for:

 - 3 -5 years of experience leading B2B SaaS sales and/or account management teams

 - Proven track record of exceeding revenue targets in a quota-carrying role

 - People Management experience — coaching, driving performance improvement, and enforcing clear accountability.

 - Strong understanding of outbound pipeline generation and funnel management

 - Data-driven mindset with the ability to track KPIs, interpret trends, and translate them into concrete actions that frontline salespeople implement consistently

 - Hands-on experience using AI to automate workflows and augment sales execution

 - Strong communication skills and ability to motivate a performance-driven team


WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Opportunity to reimagine the art industry while working with a talented, diverse, international team.

 - Competitive salary and equity

 - Flexible time off: our culture of trust and empowerment allows employees to take the time they need away from work while still excelling in their roles

 - We are a global hybrid team: most employees work from our NYC, London, or Berlin offices 2-3 days per week. (In-person expectations vary by role)

 - Private healthcare & benefit options, including: medical, dental, enhanced parental leave, Pension Plan, life assurance, Employee Assistance Programme featuring free mental health support, physio, and more

 - Professional development, including: mentoring, lunch & learns, regular training, 1:1 management, and an open feedback culture

 - Engaging opportunities and internal programming globally, to foster new relationships and build upon our collaborative community. Including: happy hours, holiday parties, global "All Hands" meetings, "Artsy Salon" (a yearly exhibition of our team's creativity), attendance to art world events, and more

The base salary range for this role is £52,000 - 72,000 annually. In addition, you will be eligible for the company’s commission plan of $40,000 at full target attainment.

All compensation pa

source_jobs.title: Senior Social Media Manager
source_jobs.description:
Artsy is looking for a Social Media Manager to help bring the art world to life across our social channels. This is a highly creative, hands-on role for someone who loves making content, is excited by visual storytelling, and wants to stay closely connected to what’s happening in New York’s galleries, museums, fairs and artist studios.

You’ll be creating social-first content from the ground up while helping shape how Artsy shows up in cultural conversations. From exhibition walk-throughs and art fair moments to timely trends and campaign storytelling, you’ll play a key role in translating the art world into engaging, accessible social content for a global audience.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of content creation, culture, and community and who’s excited to grow their voice and craft within a collaborative marketing team.





TEAM CONTEXT

This role reports to Artsy’s Social Media Lead within the Growth & Lifecycle Marketing (GLM) team, part of the broader B2C Marketing organization. You’ll work closely with colleagues across Content, Brand, GLM, and Sales to support multi-channel campaigns that drive brand awareness and engagement - while staying deeply plugged into the rhythm of the art world in London and beyond.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Create social-first, platform-native content with a strong focus on short-form, lo-fi (iPhone) video - capturing exhibitions, events, studio visits, campaigns, and timely art-world moments

 - Manage Artsy’s day-to-day social presence, including scheduling and publishing across platforms

 - Write engaging captions that reflect Artsy’s brand voice and make art feel accessible, exciting, and relevant

 - Collaborate on organic and paid social campaigns by organising assets, supporting rollouts and helping bring larger marketing initiatives to life on social

 - Support content development by coordinating outreach and logistics with artists, galleries, and creators for approved content ideas and partnerships

 - Work cross-functionally with marketing and sales colleagues to ensure social content aligns with broader campaigns and company goals

 - Stay on top of social, art, and culture trends and proactively pitch ideas for new formats, experiments, and creative approaches

 - Track performance and surface insights from social analytics to help refine content and improve engagement over time


THIS ROLE IS A FIT IF YOU…

 - Have at least 3-5 years of social media management experience, overseeing schedule management and content creation

 - Love creating social content and have hands-on experience shooting and editing social-first video

 - Have experience supporting or managing social media accounts for a brand, publication, or creative organisation

 - Are highly organised and comfortable juggling multiple projects, deadlines, and content streams

 - Have a strong sense of voice and enjoy writing clear, engaging copy tailored to different platforms

 - Are curious, collaborative and excited to learn through feedback and experimentation

 - Are interested in art, culture, or creative industries or are excited to learn more by being immersed in them

 - Are authorized to live and work in the UK


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE


IN YOUR FIRST 3 MONTHS, YOU WILL:

 - Get up to speed on Artsy’s brand voice, platforms, and social workflows

 - Confidently support day-to-day social operations, including scheduling, publishing, and asset coordination

 - Begin creating social-first video content using established Artsy formats

 - Build strong working relationships with Marketing, Editorial, Growth, and Brand stakeholders to support campaign delivery


BY 6 MONTHS, YOU WILL:

 - Regularly create and publish engaging, on-brand social content tied to campaigns and cultural moments

 - Actively contribute creative ideas that help evolve Artsy’s social presence

 - Begin tracking and reporting on social performance metrics, using insights to inform content optimisations

 -

source_jobs.title: Advertising and Partnerships Lead
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for an Advertising & Partnerships Lead to grow strategic partnerships across the global art ecosystem. This role is designed for someone deeply embedded in the art world who can open doors immediately — someone who has and can build trusted relationships with senior decision-makers across galleries, art fairs, museums, and art-adjacent businesses. This is a revenue carrying role with clear performance targets tied to advertising, data product growth, and subscriptions.

This role sits within Artsy and Artnet’s Expansion team which is responsible for growing revenue across both brands—deepening existing partner relationships while developing new opportunities across the art ecosystem. You will be responsible for selling and scaling our advertising offerings to art businesses (incl. galleries, museums, art fairs, and other related businesses), and cross-selling new and existing products across Artsy and Artnet's partner bases. You'll have the opportunity to help re-define the playbook and shape how we position our brands to partners and cultural institutions globally.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Build and grow partnerships with galleries, museums, art fairs, and art-adjacent businesses across advertising and our portfolio of commercial products.

 - Leverage your existing network within the art world to create immediate commercial opportunities and accelerate partnership growth.

 - Own the full sales cycle from prospecting and relationship development through negotiation, closing, and renewal.

 - Develop strategic, consultative relationships with senior stakeholders across the art ecosystem.

 - Identify cross-sell opportunities across our product suite as the business evolves—from advertising and intelligence tools to entirely new offerings in development.


THIS ROLE IS A FIT FOR YOU IF:

 - You have 7+ experience in advertising sales, digital media partnerships, or sponsorship sales, ideally within the art, luxury, culture, or lifestyle sectors.

 - You bring an established network within the contemporary art world and can immediately engage senior decision-makers across leading galleries, museums, fairs, and related institutions.

 - You are highly relationship-oriented but also comfortable operating in a metrics-driven environment with clear pipeline and revenue accountability.

 - You understand how brand storytelling, audience engagement, and visual identity contribute to successful advertising partnerships.

 - You are entrepreneurial, self-directed, and excited by the opportunity to help build and shape a growing business function.

 - You communicate with credibility and confidence across both creative and commercial stakeholders.

 - You have experience using Salesforce and sales analytics tools to manage pipeline health and performance reporting.

You are currently authorized to live and work in the United States. This role will be based in New York City, with 3+ days/week in-office.




THIS ROLE IS UNLIKELY TO BE A GOOD MATCH IF YOU:

 - Prefer highly structured environments with established playbooks rather than building processes and partnerships from the ground up.

 - Are uncomfortable balancing relationship-driven sales with increasing accountability around metrics, pipeline management, and performance reporting.

What success looks like in your first 3–6 months:

In your first 3 months:

 - Hit the ground running by activating your existing network to generate immediate partnership conversations and commercial opportunities.

 - Rapidly develop a strong understanding of Artsy and Artnet's full product suite, audience, and market positioning.

 - Build a healthy, qualified pipeline quickly, identifying high-value prospects and moving them through the sales cycle with urgency.

 - Begin closing early deals, demonstrating your ability to convert relationships into revenue.

In your first 6 months:

 - Close multiple high-impact partnerships with galleries, institutions, and art-adjacent businesses

source_jobs.title: Creative Director
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Artsy and Artnet have joined forces, bringing together the art world’s two most important online destinations under shared leadership and vision.

Both brands will continue to embrace their proven strengths: Artnet as the definitive destination for trusted journalism, transactional data, auctions, and market intelligence; Artsy as the leader in discovering and buying art through personalized recommendations, editorial storytelling, and a seamless collecting experience across web and mobile. Together, Artsy and Artnet attract over 7 million monthly visitors across 190+ countries and reach over 9 million followers across social channels.




THE ROLE:

We see brand as a core part of how people experience our product, mission, and place within the art world. We’re looking for a Creative Director to lead the evolution of Artsy and Artnet’s brands and visual identities at a pivotal moment for both companies. This role will help define how both brands show up across every touchpoint.

This role requires a leader who can think across the full spectrum of brand strategy and building—from brand architecture and positioning to visual identity, campaign development, and activation—ensuring Artsy and Artnet’s brand is expressed consistently and thoughtfully across every touchpoint. You will work closely across Growth Marketing, Content, Product, and Executive Leadership. The Creative Director will play a central role in connecting brand strategy, visual identity, and creative execution across the business. You’ll report to our VP of Marketing and Content and lead a small but high-impact creative function. This team helps define how Artsy and Artnet show up in the world — visually, strategically, and culturally.

This role is a combination of three things:

 - Strategy — you shape brand positioning and architecture

 - Craft — you have strong creative direction and cultural fluency. You make work that’s distinctive and emotionally resonant, and you can cast and direct the photographers, designers, and collaborators who help produce it.

 - Systems — you externalize your judgment into direction, principles, and systems so quality holds across a team through clear direction, scalable design systems, and AI-enabled workflows.

This is an opportunity to have an outsized impact on how the leading brands in the art world express themselves to collectors, galleries, artists, and the broader cultural community.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Lead the evolution of Artsy and Artnet’s brand and visual identity across all customer touchpoints, ensuring consistency, clarity, and creative distinction

 - Develop scalable creative systems and design frameworks that enable efficient execution across channels and teams

 - Shape and articulate Artsy and Artnet’s broader brand strategy, including positioning, storytelling, and visual expression

 - Partner closely with Product, Growth Marketing, and Content teams to bring the brand to life in cohesive and collaborative ways

 - Lead a small creative team, setting clear direction and priorities, establishing effective ways of working, thoughtfully planning resources, and ensuring high-quality execution

 - Build scalable creative systems, design frameworks, and AI-enabled workflows so the team and the wider org move with clarity, brand unity, and speed.

 - Grow a small team of 1-2 brand designers while directing contractors, freelancers, and creative partners, and working closely with Product Design


THIS ROLE IS A FIT FOR YOU IF:

 - This is a hybrid role based in NYC and requires 3 days a week in the office

 - You have significant experience leading brand and visual identity work for consumer-facing companies, agencies, or culturally relevant brands

 - Have worked both in-house and at agencies or brand studios—you’ve seen multiple brands and owned outcomes inside a team.

 - Understand and lead on the “why” not just the “what”

 - Think in systems: you can take intuitive creative judgment and turn

 - Partner closely with Product, Growth Marketing, and Content teams to bring the brand to life in cohesive and collaborative ways

 - Lead a small creative team, setting clear direction and priorities, establishing effective ways of working, thoughtfully planning resources, and ensuring high-quality execution

 - Build scalable creative systems, design frameworks, and AI-enabled workflows so the team and the wider org move with clarity, brand unity, and speed.

 - Grow a small team of 1-2 brand designers while directing contractors, freelancers, and creative partners, and working closely with Product Design


THIS ROLE IS A FIT FOR YOU IF:

 - This is a hybrid role based in London (or our NYC office) and requires 3 days a week in the office

 - You have significant experience leading brand and visual identity work for consumer-facing companies, agencies, or culturally relevant brands

 - Have worked both in-house and at agencies or brand studios—you’ve seen multiple brands and owned outcomes inside a team.

 - Understand and lead on the “why” not just the “what”

 - Think in systems: you can take intuitive cre

source_jobs.title: Employee Experience Associate (Part-time) m/w/d
source_jobs.description:
Artsy is looking for a proactive, organised Employee Experience Associate to support the day-to-day operations of our Berlin office and help create a welcoming, engaging workplace for our team. This is a part-time (20 hours per week), in-person role reporting to the Associate Director of People Operations.

You'll be responsible for ensuring the office runs smoothly, from coordinating workplace operations and external vendors to supporting onboarding and planning team events. Working closely with colleagues across the business and our shared office provider, you'll bring a hospitality mindset, strong organisational skills, and a proactive approach to solving problems and improving the employee experience.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys variety, takes pride in creating well-run workplaces, and is excited to make a meaningful impact on our culture through thoughtful operational support and engaging in-office experiences.

What You'll Do:




OFFICE OPERATIONS & EMPLOYEE EXPERIENCE

 - Own the day-to-day operations of our Berlin office, ensuring the space is welcoming, organised, and running smoothly

 - Manage office supplies and ensure snack & beverages are fully stocked

 - Coordinate external vendors and service providers to support office operations and project needs (e.g. facilities management, storage partners, and contractors).

 - Take ownership of local workplace projects and operational tasks, helping to continuously enhance the office environment and employee experience

 - Manage workplace systems and ensure the office remains well maintained, functional, and ready for employees each day

 - Coordinate onboarding logistics for local new hires


EVENTS & TEAM ENGAGEMENT

 - Plan and deliver engaging team events, celebrations, and office initiatives that strengthen culture and foster connection

 - Bring fresh ideas and creative energy to employee engagement, helping make the Berlin office an enjoyable and collaborative place to work

 - Coordinate local events from planning through execution, including occasional support outside of standard working hours where required
   


WHO YOU ARE:

 - This is a part-time, in-person position (20 hours per week) based in our Berlin shared office space

 - You are currently located in Berlin and authorised to work in Germany

 - Fluent in both German and English, with confidence communicating with employees, vendors, and external partners

 - Proactive and creative—you naturally identify opportunities to improve the workplace and enjoy bringing new ideas to life

 - Highly organised with excellent project management skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously

 - Reliable, responsive, and comfortable taking ownership of day-to-day office operations with minimal supervision

 - Detail-oriented—you enjoy keeping spaces organised, managing administrative tasks, and ensuring nothing falls through the cracks

 - Calm under pressure and able to prioritise competing requests effectively

 - A strong communicator with a hospitality mindset who enjoys creating positive experiences for colleagues

 - Passionate about building team engagement and creating an office environment where people enjoy coming to work

 - Excited to take ownership of the Berlin office and continuously improve how the workplace operates

 
 


WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Opportunity to reimagine the art industry while working with a talented, diverse, international team

 - Part-time schedule (20 hours per week), typically Monday-Wednesday: 9:30am-1:30pm, with Thursdays worked flexibly depending on business needs

 - Competitive salary and equity

 - Flexible time off: our culture of trust and empowerment allows employees to take the time they need away from work while still excelling in their roles

 - We are a global hybrid team: most employees work from our NYC, London, or Berlin offices 2-3 days per week. (In-person expectations vary by role)

 - Benefit options, including: German langu

notes: Artsy has a strong sales focus, with multiple sales roles and a sales lead position. The company also has a creative and design-oriented side, with roles like Lead Product Designer and Creative Director.
